title: Fix renderer tsc errors and wire a typecheck gate
description:
## What to build

Make `apps/desktop` pass `tsc --noEmit` cleanly and keep it that way with a workspace-level `npm run typecheck` script (shared, runtime, desktop) that is part of `npm run verify`.

Known errors to fix (found after adding `@types/react` / `@types/react-dom` to the desktop devDependencies):
- `src/renderer/views/ServerAgentFlows.tsx` (~lines 655, 677): `Cannot find name 't'` — regression from the main.tsx split; the file uses the translator without receiving it.
- `src/renderer/main.tsx` (~lines 511-512): `RefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>` passed where `RefObject<HTMLDivElement>` is expected — widen the ChatPanel prop types.
- `src/renderer/lib/servstationFormat.ts` (~lines 161-162): type predicate not assignable — align the attachment element type (optional vs required `mimeType`).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `cd apps/desktop && npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json` reports 0 errors
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` at the workspace root covers packages/shared, packages/runtime, apps/desktop and exits 0
- [ ] `npm run verify` runs the typecheck and stays green (build + tests + electron smoke)

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
close_reason: TypeScript now passes for shared, runtime, and desktop; npm run verify includes typecheck and is green.

title: Add downloadable Server Agent result files
description: Expose non-script files produced by completed Server Agent jobs as authenticated download links on the corresponding agent reply, including after conversation reload.
design: Normalize generatedFiles/files job result entries, correlate by jobId, fetch binary content through the runtime and IPC bridge, and download from the renderer via a temporary Blob URL.
acceptance_criteria: Completed jobs show one download link per non-script generated file; script artifacts are hidden; downloads use the authenticated job-file endpoint; historical conversations retain links; runtime and formatter tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented authenticated Server Agent result file downloads with filtering, history correlation, and coverage

title: Slim runtime snapshots: conversation metadata + paged history + virtualized message list
description:
## What to build

Finish the Phase-1 performance work: `RuntimeSnapshot` currently embeds every conversation with its full message list, so every snapshot broadcast and the initial renderer load transfer the entire chat history over IPC. Change the snapshot to carry conversation metadata (id, title, project, timestamps, message count, last-message preview) plus only the active conversation's recent messages, and let the renderer page older history in from the transcript store on demand. Virtualize the message list so long conversations render at constant cost.

State on disk already keeps per-conversation transcripts (JSONL), and messages in state.json are now capped at 200 per conversation; this issue makes the wire format match that reality.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Snapshot payload size no longer grows with total history size across conversations
- [ ] Scrolling up in a long conversation loads older messages on demand (no history loss)
- [ ] Long conversations render without per-token re-render of off-screen bubbles
- [ ] `npm run verify` (build + tests + electron smoke) stays green

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
close_reason: Snapshots now carry conversation metadata plus 50 recent active messages; transcript history is paged and renderer uses react-virtuoso.

title: Add GitHub Actions CI (install, build, typecheck, lint, test)
description:
## What to build

A GitHub Actions workflow (push + pull_request, windows-latest runner since the project targets Windows production) that runs: npm ci, npm run build, npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and the vitest suites for packages/runtime and apps/desktop.

The electron smoke test (`npm run smoke:electron`) needs a display-capable environment; include it only if it runs reliably on the CI runner, otherwise document that it stays a local release gate via `npm run verify`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Workflow file under .github/workflows runs green on a test PR/push
- [ ] Failure in build, typecheck, lint, or tests fails the workflow
- [ ] README or docs mention the CI gate

## Blocked by

- HyBotClient-8bo (typecheck must exist)
- HyBotClient-duf (lint must exist)
close_reason: Added .github/workflows/ci.yml for Windows push/PR verification, documented the CI gate in README, and confirmed GitHub Actions run #1 succeeded on commit 6118b9d.

title: Add permissionPolicy and toolExecutor boundary matrix tests
description:
## What to build

Direct unit tests for the two modules that decide whether local tools run: `packages/runtime/src/permissionPolicy.ts` (rule matching: exact, wildcard `mcp.*`, `*`; mode matrix: plan / acceptEdits / bypassPermissions; read vs dangerous risk) and `packages/runtime/src/toolExecutor.ts` (project boundary validation: ReadFile/WriteFile path containment, shell command heuristics, MCP path-like argument scanning, read-only workspace mode, permission timeout).

The runtime.test.ts suite covers these only indirectly through full job flows; targeted tests document the security-relevant matrix and catch regressions cheaply.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] permissionPolicy: every mode x risk x rule-behavior combination asserted
- [ ] toolExecutor: path traversal, parent-directory references, out-of-root absolute paths, and MCP path scanning each have a dedicated test
- [ ] Permission timeout path produces a denied record with a timeout message
- [ ] `cd packages/runtime && npx vitest run` stays green

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
close_reason: Added permission policy and tool executor boundary/security matrix coverage; all runtime tests pass.

title: Adopt ESLint (typescript-eslint recommended) with a lint script
description:
## What to build

Add ESLint 9 flat config at the workspace root with typescript-eslint's recommended rule set, wired as `npm run lint` and included in `npm run verify`. Add a Prettier config for editor integration, but do NOT gate on formatting and do not mass-reformat (that is tracked separately).

Scope:
- Lint `packages/*/src`, `packages/runtime/test`, `apps/desktop/src`, `scripts/*.cjs` with sensible ignores (dist, release, node_modules, build output).
- Fix or explicitly suppress the first wave of findings; keep the ruleset at 'recommended' (no opinionated extras) so the signal-to-noise ratio stays high.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `npm run lint` exits 0 across the workspace
- [ ] No inline rule disables without a short justification comment
- [ ] `npm run verify` includes lint and stays green

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
close_reason: Added ESLint flat config with recommended JavaScript/TypeScript rules and wired lint into verify.

title: Tighten main-process IPC payload types and validate at runtime
description:
## What to build

Fix the remaining `tsc` errors in `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts` where IPC handlers forward loosely-typed renderer payloads into the runtime, and add defensive runtime validation at the IPC boundary so a malformed payload fails with a clear error instead of corrupting runtime state.

Known sites (line numbers approximate):
- permission rule handler (~line 705): `behavior: string` forwarded where `PermissionRuleBehavior` is required.
- Servstation A2A config update (~line 1066): `authMode: string` forwarded where `ServstationA2AAuthMode` is required.
- Servstation send prompt (~line 1112) and scheduled job (~line 1124): `prompt: string | undefined` forwarded where `string` is required.

For each, narrow the accepted type (string literal unions / required fields) and reject invalid values early with a descriptive error.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The four IPC sites above type-check without casts to `any`
- [ ] Handlers reject out-of-range enum values and missing required strings with clear errors
- [ ] `npm run test -w hbclient` and `npm run verify` stay green

## Blocked by

- HyBotClient-8bo (typecheck gate should land first so the fixes are provable)
close_reason: Main-process IPC payload validation and narrowed payload types are implemented and verified by typecheck/tests.

title: Switch default Servstation endpoints and update feed to HTTPS once the server supports TLS
description:
## What to build

Default Servstation/Botstation endpoints currently use plain HTTP (verified: the host answers 200 on http and refuses https), so bearer tokens, OIDC tokens, mail passwords, and the electron-updater feed all cross the network unencrypted. Once the server terminates TLS: switch the shared default endpoint constants to https://, and make the update feed reject non-https URLs.

The constants were consolidated into `@supbot/shared` in this cycle, so this is a small, deliberate change — the work is the server-side TLS migration plus a staged rollout (existing installs keep their configured URLs; only defaults change).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Default endpoints in @supbot/shared use https://
- [ ] Update feed refuses non-https URLs (or fails closed with a clear error)
- [ ] Upgrade path verified: packaged app updates and OIDC login work against the TLS endpoint
- [ ] docs/production-windows.md security checklist updated

## Blocked by

None - blocked externally (server-side TLS support required)
notes: Client-side hardening is complete: the updater rejects non-HTTPS feeds with a clear error and production TLS requirements are documented. Default Servstation/OIDC/update URLs remain HTTP because the configured server still refuses HTTPS; packaged update and OIDC verification require server-side TLS before this issue can close.

title: Run Prettier across the repo and unify line endings
description:
## What to build

The codebase mixes LF and CRLF line endings within the same files (which makes naive multi-line edits error-prone) and has no enforced formatting. Run a one-time Prettier pass over all source with a single line-ending policy (recommend LF via .gitattributes), then keep it honest in CI.

HITL because this touches nearly every file and produces a huge diff — it should be scheduled when no large feature branches are in flight, and the team should confirm the line-ending policy.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] .gitattributes and Prettier config agree on the line-ending policy
- [ ] One-time format commit; `prettier --check` passes afterwards
- [ ] Format check added to CI (after HyBotClient-5q2) or to `npm run verify`
- [ ] Release verification (`npm run verify`) green after the format pass

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately (schedule around in-flight work)
close_reason: Added Prettier configuration, LF enforcement, repository formatting pass, and format check in verify.

title: Decide and implement state.json / transcript message deduplication
description:
## What to build

Chat messages are currently persisted twice: inside the monolithic state.json (capped at 200 per conversation) and in per-conversation transcript JSONL files. Decide the single source of truth (likely: transcripts own messages, state.json keeps metadata only) and implement the storage migration, including startup migration for existing installs and recovery behavior when one side is missing/corrupt.

HITL because this changes the on-disk format for existing users and needs a decision on migration + rollback strategy.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Decision recorded (which store owns messages, migration + rollback approach)
- [ ] Existing state.json files migrate without message loss
- [ ] Startup recovers cleanly from a corrupt state.json or transcript (storage .bak fallback already exists)
- [ ] Runtime tests cover the migration path

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
close_reason: Made transcripts the message source of truth, migrated legacy state messages, documented rollback/recovery, and added migration tests.
